coredata.dc:title: Novel Feedback-Bayesian BP Neural Network Combined with Extended Kalman Filtering for the Battery State-of-Charge Estimation
coredata.prism:copyright: Copyright © 2021 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.prism:publisher: The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.dc:creator: Zhang, Yixing
Wang, Shunli
Xu, Wenhua
Fernandez, Carlos
Fan, Yongcun
coredata.dc:description: The state of charge estimation of lithium-ion batteries plays an important role in real-time monitoring and safety. To solve the problem that high non-linearity during real-time estimation of lithium-ion batteries who cause that it is difficult to estimate accurately. Taking lithium-ion battery as the research object, the working characteristics of lithium-ion ion battery are studied under various working conditions. To reduce the error caused by the nonlinearity of the lithium battery system, the BP neural network with the high approximation of nonlinearity is combined with the extended Kalman filtering. At the same time, to eliminate the overfitting of training, Bayesian regularization is used to optimize the neural network. Taking into account the real-time requirements of lithium-ion batteries, a feedback network is adopted to carry out real-time algorithm integration on lithium-ion batteries. A simulation model is established, and the results are analyzed in combination with various working conditions. Experimental results show that the algorithm has the characteristics of fast convergence and good tracking effect, and the estimation error is within 1.10%. It is verified that the Feedback-Bayesian BP neural network combined with the extended Kalman filtering algorithm can improve the accuracy of lithium-ion battery state-of-charge estimation.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Feedback-Bayesian
Non-linearity
BP neural network
Extended Kalman filtering
State of charge

xocs:meta.xocs:articleinfo: rawtext articleinfo articlenumber articletitlenorm authfirstinitialnorm authfirstsurnamenorm cid cids contenttype copyright crossmark dateloaded dateloadedtxt datesearch datesort dateupdated dco docsubtype doctype doi eid ewtransactionid hubeid indexeddate issfirst issn issnnorm issuelist itemstage itemtransactionid itemweight oauserlicense openaccess openarchive pg pgfirst pii piinorm pubdateend pubdatestart pubdatetxt pubyr sortorder srctitle srctitlenorm srctype ssids alllist content oa subj tomb volfirst volissue volumelist webpdf webpdfpagecount yearnav affil articletitle auth authfirstini authfull authkeywords authlast primabst ref
xocs:meta.xocs:copyright-line: Copyright © 2021 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.

xocs:doc.xocs:rawtext: Novel Feedback-Bayesian BP Neural Network Combined with Extended Kalman Filtering for the Battery State-of-Charge Estimation Yixing Zhang, 1 Shunli Wang, 1 497420789@qq.com Wenhua Xu, 1 Carlos Fernandez, 2 Yongcun Fan, 1 1 School of Information Engineering, Southwest University of Science and Technology, Mianyang, 621010, China School of Information Engineering Southwest University of Science and Technology Mianyang 621010 China School of Information Engineering, Southwest University of Science and Technology, Mianyang, 621010, China 2 School of Pharmacy and Life Sciences, Robert Gordon University, Aberdeen AB17GJ, UK. School of Pharmacy and Life Sciences Robert Gordon University Aberdeen AB17GJ UK School of Pharmacy and Life Sciences, Robert Gordon University, Aberdeen AB17GJ, UK. The state of charge estimation of lithium-ion batteries plays an important role in real-time monitoring and safety. To solve the problem that high non-linearity during real-time estimation of lithium-ion batteries who cause that it is difficult to estimate accurately. Taking lithium-ion battery as the research object, the working characteristics of lithium-ion ion battery are studied under various working conditions. To reduce the error caused by the nonlinearity of the lithium battery system, the BP neural network with the high approximation of nonlinearity is combined with the extended Kalman filtering. At the same time, to eliminate the overfitting of training, Bayesian regularization is used to optimize the neural network. Taking into account the real-time requirements of lithium-ion batteries, a feedback network is adopted to carry out real-time algorithm integration on lithium-ion batteries. A simulation model is established, and the results are analyzed in combination with various working conditions. Experimental results show that the algorithm has the characteristics of fast convergence and good tracking effect, and the estimation error is within 1.10%. It is verified that the Feedback-Bayesian BP neural network combined with the extended Kalman filtering algorithm can improve the accuracy of lithium-ion battery state-of-charge estimation. Keywords Feedback-Bayesian Non-linearity BP neural network Extended Kalman filtering State of charge References 1. B.H. Peng, X. Sheng, G. Wei, Environ Sci Pollut R 27: (2020) 39135-B. H. Peng, X. Sheng and G. Wei, Environ Sci Pollut R, 27 (2020) 39135. 2. T.K. Burki, Lancet Resp Med 8: (2020) 549-T. K. Burki, Lancet Resp Med, 8 (2020) 549. 3. Y.H. Zhao, X.L. Zhang, Y. Wang, Environ Sci Pollut R 27: (2020) 28333-Y. H. Zhao, X. L. Zhang and Y. Wang, Environ Sci Pollut R, 27 (2020) 28333. 4. R. Basso, B. Kulcsar, I. Sanchez-Diaz, Transport Res B-Meth 145: (2021) 24-R. Basso, B. Kulcsar and I. Sanchez-Diaz, Transport Res B-Meth, 145 (2021) 24. 5. M. Luzi, F. M.F. Mascioli, M. Paschero, A. Rizzi, Ieee T Neur Net Lear 31: (2020) 371-M. Luzi, F. M. F. Mascioli, M. Paschero and A. Rizzi, Ieee T Neur Net Lear, 31 (2020) 371. 6. D.X. Xiao, G.L. Fang, S. Liu, S.Y. Yuan, R. Ahmed, S. Habibi, A. Emadi, Ieee T Power Electr 35: (2020) 12332-D. X. Xiao, G. L. Fang, S. Liu, S. Y. Yuan, R. Ahmed, S. Habibi and A. Emadi, Ieee T Power Electr, 35 (2020) 12332. 7. Z.H. Cen, P. Xie, T. Yao, Ieee T Transp Electr (2021) 1-J. Xie and T. Yao, Ieee T Transp Electr, (2021) 1. 12. L. Feng, J. Ding, Y.Y. Han, Ionics 26: (2020) 2875-L. Feng, J. Ding and Y. Y. Han, Ionics, 26 (2020) 2875. 13. Z.X. Liu, Z. Li, J.B. Zhang, L.S. Su, H. Ge, Energies 12: (2019) Z. X. Liu, Z. Li, J. B. Zhang, L. S. Su and H. Ge, Energies, 12 (2019). 14. L.L. Li, Z.F. Liu, C.H. Gao, Int J Energ Res 44: (2020) 10262-Z. Q. Lyu and R. J. Gao, Int J Energ Res, 44 (2020) 10262. 28. C. Jiang, S.L. Wang, B. Wu, B. Etse-Dabu, X. Xiong, Int J Electrochem Sc 15: (2020) 9720-C. Jiang, S. L. Wang, B. Wu, B. Etse-Dabu and X. Xiong, Int J Electrochem Sc, 15 (2020) 9720. 29. Y. He, Q. Li, X.X. Zheng, X.T. Liu, J Power Electron 21: (2021) 590-Y. He, Q. Li, X. X. Zheng and X. T. head.ce:title: Novel Feedback-Bayesian BP Neural Network Combined with Extended Kalman Filtering for the Battery State-of-Charge Estimation

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Pharmacy and Life Sciences

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Pharmacy and Life Sciences, Robert Gordon University, Aberdeen AB17GJ, UK.

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: The state of charge estimation of lithium-ion batteries plays an important role in real-time monitoring and safety. To solve the problem that high non-linearity during real-time estimation of lithium-ion batteries who cause that it is difficult to estimate accurately. Taking lithium-ion battery as the research object, the working characteristics of lithium-ion ion battery are studied under various working conditions. To reduce the error caused by the nonlinearity of the lithium battery system, the BP neural network with the high approximation of nonlinearity is combined with the extended Kalman filtering. At the same time, to eliminate the overfitting of training, Bayesian regularization is used to optimize the neural network. Taking into account the real-time requirements of lithium-ion batteries, a feedback network is adopted to carry out real-time algorithm integration on lithium-ion batteries. A simulation model is established, and the results are analyzed in combination with various working conditions. Experimental results show that the algorithm has the characteristics of fast convergence and good tracking effect, and the estimation error is within 1.10%. It is verified that the Feedback-Bayesian BP neural network combined with the extended Kalman filtering algorithm can improve the accuracy of lithium-ion battery state-of-charge estimation.
